summary: Crisp soundboard capture of a classic ’74 show highlighted by a smoking Dark Star→Slipknot! and tight Brown‑Eyed Women.
review: The recording is a high‑fidelity soundboard tape that most reviewers describe as crisp, clear and “great AUD”, with only minor distortion on a couple of tracks. Musically the concert shines – Dark Star segues into an inspired Slipknot! jam, Brown‑Eyed Women and Row Jimmy are tight, and the second set flows beautifully through Scarlet, Jack Straw and a stunning Ship Of Fools encore. Overall the performance is praised as quintessential 1974 Dead with just a few quirky moments.

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviews (over 15) give 4‑5 stars and highlight both superb musical moments and excellent soundboard quality, with only isolated minor complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Consistently described as crisp, clear and great AUD; a few reviewers note slight distortion on One More Saturday Night and U.S. Blues but no major drop‑outs or static.

show_quality.setlist_flow: Typical 1974 setlist with a solid first set and an especially adventurous second set; the Dark Star > Slipknot! transition anchors the jam‑heavy middle, leading into a tight closer.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Praised for sweet slide on Row Jimmy, strong vocal delivery (e.g., “Come Back Trippin’”), and inventive improvisation in Dark Star.
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted for great bass tone on Brown‑Eyed Women and taking a lead role vocally/bassically on Jack Straw.
band_member_comments.Keys: Keith’s electric piano work highlighted as “plinky winky” on Row Jimmy and creative ideas during Dark Star.
